Resolves #11882

Authored by GPT-5 (Codex). Session 7fa9a0db-5c77-4584-b7a5-7693c7ef90c0.

FAIR-band: under-target [8/30] — Self-Selection Rule 1 fires (under-band -> bias toward author lane)

Adds row-level Surface-Anchor V-B-A to the Contract Ledger authoring guide, then wires compact pointers into ticket-create, ticket-intake, and pr-review workflow payloads. The rule stays out of AGENTS.md and avoids a new skill: detailed guidance lives in the Contract Ledger reference, while workflow files only fire the narrow check when a Contract Ledger row names an existing surface.

Evidence: L1 (static docs/workflow audit + skill-manifest lint + exact grep) -> L1 required (documentation/workflow substrate ACs). No residuals.

## Deltas from ticket

- Kept pr-review compact by folding surface-anchor failures into the existing Contract drift Required Action path instead of adding a separate long review subsection.
- Added the concrete #11789/#11790/#11791 failure anchors directly in `learn/agentos/contract-ledger.md`: `sourceFiles`, `resolveDeploymentEnabled()`, `healthcheck.orchestrator.tasks`, nonexistent `setTenantConfig` MCP-tool framing, and ADR 0014 `tenant-repo-sync` / GitMirror scope drift.

## Slot Rationale

- `learn/agentos/contract-ledger.md` new subsection: disposition `keep` in conditionally loaded reference; trigger-frequency Contract Ledger authoring/review only; failure-severity medium-high because stale contract rows cause multi-cycle PR/review friction; enforceability discipline-first with grep/static review evidence.
- `ticket-create` pointer: disposition `compress-to-trigger`; one sentence keeps the authoring map small while routing detail to Contract Ledger reference.
- `ticket-intake` pointer: disposition `compress-to-trigger`; one readiness-gate bullet lets intake block stale row anchors without duplicating examples.
- `pr-review` pointer: disposition `compress-to-trigger`; folds surface-anchor drift into existing Contract drift audit to avoid review payload bloat.
- No AGENTS.md expansion and no new skill: the failure mode is Contract Ledger-specific, not universal per-turn substrate.

### 🔬 Depth Floor

**Challenge (per guide §7.1):**

**Non-blocking observability concern (mention-worthy):** The 5 failure-class list captures author-time drift modes (where a ledger row diverges from current substrate at WRITE time). It doesn't enumerate **evolving-substrate drift** — where a ledger row was substrate-correct at write time but becomes stale during the implementation cycle when sibling subs ship surface changes. E.g., a Contract Ledger written in week 1 may cite `helperA` correctly, then week 2's PR renames `helperA → helperB`, leaving the week-1 ledger silently stale. Today this surfaces via Drift Detection at PR-review time (per `pr-review-guide.md §5.4`), but not at ticket-intake time when an implementer picks up an aged ticket. Forward-compat note: if surface-anchor drift starts surfacing in ticket-intake reviews after pickup-delay tickets, consider adding "intake-time re-V-B-A on aged tickets" as a 6th failure class. Today's 5 classes cover the most common patterns at this nightshift's substrate state.

**Rhetorical-Drift Audit (per guide §7.4):** Pass. The "narrower than verify everything and broader than check field names" framing accurately scopes the discipline — it's specifically about EXISTING-surface rows (subset of all ledger rows, since new-surface rows are explicitly framed as new). PR body description matches the diff exactly. No `[RETROSPECTIVE]` claim to audit. Linked-anchor citations check out (the 5 failure classes are each traceable to specific commits/refinements I introduced friction for).
